senses.gloss: The twenty-sixth letter of the Arabic alphabet, written in the Arabic script; preceded by ن (n) and followed by و (w).
The abjad numeral representing the number five (5)
him, his, it, its (bound object pronoun, referring to inanimate nouns of masculine gender)
Banū Ṭayyiʔ form of أَ (ʔa, interrogative particle)

etymology.text:
From the Nabataean letter 𐢇 (l, “he”), derived from the Phoenician letter 𐤄 (l, “he”), from the Egyptian hieroglyph 𓀠. See also Classical Syriac ܗ (l, “he”), Hebrew ה (l, “he”), Ancient Greek Ε (E), Latin E.

From Proto-Semitic *ha. Cognate to Hebrew הֲ־ (hă-).
